Content_Sketch: Handshake in European cafe - warm smile across the table, coffee, cobblestone outside the window. You know their real name. They do not know yours.
Illustration_Note: Warm cafe interior, two stickmen at small table, handshake, window showing European street

Content_Sketch: Weight statement: you have never told anyone who you are. Not even the person sitting next to you every day. The cost of two lives.
Illustration_Note: Bold white text on dark: 'They trust you. That is how you win.'

Content_Sketch: Age 21. Elite IDF unit. You are noticed by someone you never see. A phone call with no organization name. An invitation you cannot refuse.
Illustration_Note: Young stickman in olive IDF uniform, holding phone, military base background

Content_Sketch: Months of psychological screening. Simulations under assumed identities. Stress tests that strip you down. They want to know if you can become someone else.
Illustration_Note: Stickman in civilian clothes at interview table, two faceless evaluators across, stark room

Content_Sketch: Midrasha academy near Herzliya. Two years. Surveillance detection, source recruitment, cover management. You learn to lie with your whole body.
Illustration_Note: Training facility exterior, group of stickmen in dark civilian clothes, Herzliya cityscape

Content_Sketch: Final exercise: three weeks in a foreign city under a fake name. No one knows who you are. By the end you respond to the fake name without thinking. You graduate. No diploma. No photo.
Illustration_Note: Stickman walking alone on European street with bag, nighttime, new identity documents in hand

Content_Sketch: Deployed to a European capital. New identity: businessman. An apartment with someone else's name on the lease. A cafe where you become a regular.
Illustration_Note: Stickman in blazer entering warm-lit cafe, European architecture, apartment building visible

Content_Sketch: First target: a man with access. You meet by coincidence - except nothing is coincidence. Coffee. Then dinner. Then birthdays. You build trust brick by brick.
Illustration_Note: Two stickmen at restaurant table, wine glasses, stickman with glasses (target) talking warmly

Content_Sketch: Target starts to share private things - family fears, childhood memories, his daughter's school. You listen. You nod. You file every word into a report marked CLASSIFIED.
Illustration_Note: Close view of two stickmen talking, SUBJECT writing in notebook later at safe house desk, warm vs cold split

Content_Sketch: Phone call home to Israel. Your wife's voice. Your daughter asks when Abba is coming home. You say soon. You have said soon for eleven months.
Illustration_Note: Stickman holding phone in dark apartment, window showing European city at night, isolated

Content_Sketch: Sayanim network: a stranger gives you a car. Another gives you a room. Documents appear. No one asks why. You live in a shadow infrastructure built on loyalty you cannot repay.
Illustration_Note: Stickman receiving car keys from faceless figure in parking garage, no words exchanged

Content_Sketch: Target invites you to dinner at his home. His wife cooks. His daughter - same age as yours - runs circles around the table laughing.
Illustration_Note: Dinner table scene, target family + SUBJECT, warm lighting, child running, wine being poured

Content_Sketch: Phone buzzes in your pocket. Code word: Activate. You keep smiling. You keep talking. No one at the table notices anything. You pour yourself more wine.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT at dinner table, phone glow from pocket, frozen smile, everyone else laughing

Content_Sketch: You could refuse. You could stand up and tell the truth. No gun to your head. No immediate threat. This is a choice. And you choose to continue.
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT's face close-up, dot eyes fixed, hand on wine glass, deliberate stillness

Content_Sketch: You extract what you need. You say goodnight at the door. They hug you. The next morning your apartment is empty. Your phone number no longer exists. Two weeks later you read the news.
Illustration_Note: Split: left - goodbye hug at door; right - empty apartment, stripped clean, morning light

Content_Sketch: Next mission. Then the next. You stop counting. Faces blur. Names blur. Only the wristwatch stays the same.
Illustration_Note: Montage: multiple cafe meetings, different cities, same blazer, same watch, faces changing

Content_Sketch: Back in Israel between missions. Your daughter runs to her mother instead of you. Your wife says she does not know who you are anymore. Neither do you.
Illustration_Note: Israeli apartment, daughter running past SUBJECT to mother, harsh white sunlight, empty feeling

Content_Sketch: Leave Mossad. No ceremony. No medal. No one says thank you. You sit in a Tel Aviv cafe and someone calls a name. You flinch. Which name?
Illustration_Note: SUBJECT in grey shirt at Tel Aviv cafe, flinching, bright daylight, alone at table

Content_Sketch: Therapist asks what trauma you experienced. You are not allowed to answer. You Google your old target's name. Social media still there. Family photos. Smiles. You close the laptop.
Illustration_Note: Split: therapist office (SUBJECT silent) / laptop screen showing social media photos, then closed

Content_Sketch: Another cafe. Another person across from you. But this time no mission. No handler. No file. Your hands still scan exits and count faces by reflex.
Illustration_Note: Tel Aviv cafe, SUBJECT in grey shirt, coffee, watching the room, wristwatch visible, quiet

Content_Sketch: You lived two lives. And now you do not know which one was real. If you ever wondered what it costs to build trust for a living - now you know.
Illustration_Note: Bold white text on dark: 'You lived two lives. And now you do not know which one was real.'
